name: Seychelles

colors.symbolism: the sky and the sea that surrounds Seychelles
the sun, source of light and life
the people and their determination to work for the future in unity and love
the land and the natural environment

description: Five oblique bands radiating from the lower-hoist corner — blue, yellow, red, white and green from top to bottom — fanning out across the flag to the upper and fly edges.
history: Adopted on 18 June 1996. It replaced the 1977 flag of the Second Republic (red over green divided by a wavy white band, of socialist inspiration) following the country's transition to multi-party democracy in 1993. The five colours combine those of the country's two largest political parties, intentionally signalling national reconciliation. The current flag was designed by a national commission and is unusual in its dynamic, fan-like rather than rectangular composition.

features.symbolism: five oblique bands of blue, yellow, red, white and green radiate from the lower-hoist corner to the upper and fly edges, evoking a young nation moving forward into a bright future. The five colours are the colours of the two main political parties at the time the flag was adopted (blue and yellow for the Seychelles Democratic Party; red, white and green for the Seychelles People's Progressive Front), symbolising national reconciliation
